name: Dog Breeds
description: Name 65 dog breeds from photos, from the Labrador to the Basenji. Extras give each breed's giveaway field marks, its story, and how to split the classic confusion pairs: Husky vs Malamute, Whippet vs Greyhound, Malinois vs German Shepherd, Westie vs Scottie.

fields.extra: America's long-reigning favorite. Solid black, yellow, or chocolate; blocky head, otter tail, permanently food-motivated. Vs Golden: the Lab's coat is short and dense, never feathered.
Scottish bird dog with a feathered gold coat and a perpetual grin. Vs Lab: if the coat is long, wavy, and golden with feathering on legs and tail, it is a Golden.
Black-and-tan with a black saddle, upright ears, and a famously sloped back. Vs Belgian Malinois: the Malinois is lighter, squarer, and solid fawn with a black mask.
Bat ears are the giveaway: tall, rounded, always up. Compact, flat-faced, under 30 pounds. English lace workers took their toy bulldogs to France; Paris made them famous.
Curly, non-shedding coat in three sizes (standard, miniature, toy). Originally a German duck retriever; the show clip's pom-poms once kept joints warm in cold water.
The English original: low-slung, massive underslung jaw, loose facial wrinkles, rolling sailor's gait. Vs Frenchie: much bigger, with rose ears folded back, never bat ears.
Small tricolor scent hound with long dropped ears and a white-tipped tail held high like a flag. The airport-security sniffer of choice; famously loud bay.
The sausage dog: long body, short legs, in smooth, long, or wire coats. German for 'badger dog'; the shape is for following badgers down their burrows.
Powerful black dog with sharply defined rust markings on cheeks, chest, and legs. Descended from Roman drover dogs via the German town of Rottweil.
Medium sled dog, often blue-eyed, with a masked face and a brush tail. Vs Alaskan Malamute: the Husky is smaller and sleeker; Malamutes are heavyweights with brown eyes.
The Queen's breed: fox head, big upright ears, long low body, and effectively no tail. A Corgi WITH a long tail is the Cardigan variety.
The world's smallest breed, from the Mexican state of the same name. Apple-domed skull, enormous ears, and the confidence of a much larger animal.
Small, solid, flat-faced, with a tightly curled tail and a black mask on fawn. Ancient Chinese lapdog. Vs Frenchie: the Pug's ears fold down and its tail curls.
The workaholic genius of dogdom, usually black-and-white with a low, stalking gait and an intense stare ('the eye'). Dominates every agility and obedience podium.
Merle patchwork coat and often two different eye colors; tail usually bobbed. Despite the name, developed on American ranches. Vs Border Collie: flashier coat, stockier build.
Tiny, with a long silky steel-blue-and-tan coat and a topknot. Bred to kill rats in Yorkshire mills before graduating to handbags.
Square-jawed German guard dog in fawn or brindle with a black mask and often white chest and feet. Famous for standing on hind legs and 'boxing' with its front paws.
The apartment-blocking giant; one of the tallest breeds on earth. German, not Danish (Germans call it the Deutsche Dogge). Scooby-Doo is one.
The only spotted breed: white with round black or liver spots. Born pure white; the spots come in later. Historic carriage and firehouse dog.
Tibetan-Chinese palace dog with a flat face, flowing double coat, and a proud strut; hair often tied in a topknot. The name means 'lion dog'.
A pointed fox face inside an absurd orange puffball, tail plumed over the back. A downsized spitz; Queen Victoria drove the miniaturization.
'The American Gentleman': black-and-white tuxedo markings, rounded erect ears, gentle underbite. Vs Frenchie: leggier, always tuxedo-patterned, ears rounder.
Melting dark eyes, long feathered ears, silky coat; the chestnut-on-white pattern is called Blenheim. A toy spaniel named for King Charles II, who was never without them.
Sleek black-and-rust guard dog built like an athlete; ears often cropped erect, tail docked. Bred by German tax collector Louis Dobermann for protection on his rounds. US spelling: Doberman Pinscher.
Small, salt-and-pepper, with the signature beard and bushy eyebrows of a tiny disapproving professor. The most popular of the three Schnauzer sizes.
Large, calm Swiss farm dog: jet black with rust points and white blaze, chest, and paws. Vs Saint Bernard: the Bernese is tricolor black; Saints are red-and-white.
Massive red-and-white Alpine rescue dog with a huge head and famously drooly jowls. Bred by monks at the Great St Bernard Pass; the brandy barrel is a myth.
Long, low, and mournful: heavy bones, trailing ears, sad eyes, short crooked legs. A scent hound built for slow, relentless trailing. Vs Beagle: far lower and droopier.
The best nose on earth: loose wrinkled skin, enormous swinging ears, solemn expression, usually black-and-tan or red. Its trailing evidence has stood up in court.
The fastest dog: 45 mph. Deep chest, wasp waist, tiny folded ears, and a professional couch-potato temperament off the track. Vs Whippet: much taller.
A half-size Greyhound with the same aerodynamic curves. Vs Greyhound: knee-height rather than hip-height; vs Italian Greyhound: the Whippet is the middle size.
Small, mostly white, endlessly bouncing fox-hunting terrier in smooth or rough coat. Named for Reverend John Russell; the AKC show version is the Parson Russell.
The Westie: a small, all-white Scottish terrier with dark button eyes and pricked ears. Vs Scottie: same silhouette family, but the Westie is only ever white.
The Scottie of whisky labels and Monopoly: nearly always black, with a long head, beard, and upright dagger tail. Vs Westie: the dark one of the pair.
Ancient Mediterranean lapdog draped in a straight, silky, floor-length white coat with dark button eyes. Vs Bichon: silk, not powder-puff curls.
A white powder-puff: dense curly coat groomed into a rounded cloud, with a plumed tail over the back. Vs Maltese: curls, not silk. Vs Poodle: smaller, rounder face.
Cuba's national dog: small, sturdy, with a long wavy coat in ANY color and a springy gait. Vs Maltese/Bichon: the one that comes in colors other than white.
French for 'butterfly': huge fringed ears set like spread wings on a dainty white-and-color body. One of the smartest of all toy breeds.
Japan's small fox-like spitz: red coat with cream trim, curled tail, and a cat's independence. The face of the 'doge' meme. Vs Akita: the Shiba is a fraction of the size.
Japan's large, bear-headed spitz with a big curled tail and quiet, dignified loyalty; faithful Hachiko was one. Vs Shiba: same look, triple the dog.
Brilliant white Siberian sled and reindeer dog wearing a permanent upturned 'Sammy smile' (which stops drool from freezing). A walking cloud that sheds accordingly.
The heavy freight hauler of the Arctic: bigger and bulkier than a Husky, always brown-eyed, with a plumed tail carried over the back. Blue eyes rule OUT Malamute.
An ancient Chinese breed resembling a scowling lion, with a huge mane, stilted gait, and a unique blue-black tongue. Aloof with strangers, cat-like at home.
Deep folded wrinkles, a 'hippopotamus' muzzle, tiny ears, and (like the Chow) a blue-black tongue. Ancient Chinese guard and fighting dog; the name means 'sand skin'.
The Sheltie: looks exactly like a miniature Rough Collie, with a full mane and blaze of white. Vs Rough Collie: knee-height instead of thigh-height.
Lassie's breed: elegant, long-coated Scottish herder with a narrow refined muzzle and sable-and-white coat. Vs Sheltie: the full-size version.
A gray-and-white shag carpet with a bear's rolling gait, hair over its eyes, and no visible tail (the 'Bobtail'). The Dulux paint dog.
The Blue Heeler: compact, mottled blue or red all over, bred with Dingo blood to drive cattle by nipping heels. Routinely tops dog-longevity records.
The military and police favorite: fawn with a black mask and ears, lighter and squarer than a German Shepherd, with a flat back and turbocharged drive. Navy SEALs jump with them.
Muscular, short-coated, broad-cheeked, in any color. 'Pit bull' on the street is an umbrella covering this breed, the American Staffordshire Terrier, and their mixes.
The gamekeeper's night dog (60% Mastiff, 40% Bulldog): huge, fawn or brindle, with a black mask. Bred to silently knock down poachers and hold them.
Italian mastiff-guardian: enormous but athletic, short gray, black, or fawn coat, often cropped ears. The name (KAH-neh KOR-so) is Latin-rooted: 'bodyguard dog'.
A gentle black (or brown, or landseer black-and-white) giant with webbed feet and a waterproof coat, bred to haul nets and rescue drowning sailors. Vs Bernese: no rust markings.
All-white livestock guardian that lives with its flock and treats wolves as a personnel problem. Vs Newfoundland: white; vs Samoyed: giant-size and calm.
Wheaten African lion-tracking hound named for its signature: a ridge of hair growing BACKWARD along the spine. Vs Vizsla: bigger, with the ridge.
The 'Gray Ghost': sleek silver-gray German hunting dog with amber or blue-gray eyes. Vs Vizsla: gray coat instead of rust.
Hungarian pointer in solid rust from nose to tail: even the nose and eyes match the coat. A velcro dog that shadows its owner. Vs Weimaraner: rust, not gray.
Liver-and-white bird dog, usually with a solid liver head and a ticked/roan body. The all-purpose hunter and a perennial field-trial champion.
Compact orange-and-white French pointing dog, leggy and high-energy, with minimal tail. Once 'Brittany Spaniel', but it points like a setter, so 'Spaniel' was dropped.
Medium liver-and-white or black-and-white spaniel with a soft expression and long feathered ears; it 'springs' game from cover. Vs Cocker: bigger, longer muzzle.
The smallest sporting breed: dome head, short upturned muzzle, huge pleading eyes, and a floor-sweeping show coat. Lady in 'Lady and the Tramp'.
Aristocratic bird dog in a flowing mahogany-red coat, all elegance and enthusiasm. Vs Vizsla: taller, with a long silky feathered coat.
Curly or wavy non-shedding fisherman's dog that herded fish into nets; tail often shaved to a lion clip. The Obamas' Bo made it famous. Vs Poodle: sturdier, rectangular.
'The King of Terriers': the largest terrier, black-and-tan with a wiry coat, sporting beard, and folded ears. Served as a WWI messenger dog.
The barkless dog of central Africa: it yodels instead. Small, chestnut-and-white, with a wrinkled forehead, pricked ears, and a tight ring tail; grooms itself like a cat.
